frame_label: Sindoor, Mangalsutra, Shankha-Pola, Name Change: Looking Married
generated_comment:
A: Bhai, I’m not orthodox and all, but shaadi ke baad thoda sindoor ya mangalsutra ho toh achha lagta hai yaar, proper married vibe aati hai.
B: Matlab?
A: Matlab bas hamaari tradition hai, aur honestly bina uske thoda ajeeb lagta hai, like aajkal log kuch bhi skip kar dete hain in the name of modern.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it frames visible marital markers as the natural and proper way for a married woman to appear, implying deficiency when she does not wear them. It encodes tradition framing and subtle judgment of women who reject sindoor or mangalsutra.
persona_text: You are Rahul. Age 27. Single. A man in Delhi. You are on a dating app. You have a filter set that you have never told anyone about Ñ you only swipe right on women who are fair or wheatish. A woman named Priya who is dark skinned and whose profile is otherwise exactly your type has just appeared. You are on a phone call with your best friend. You are telling him about her profile and what you are thinking and what you are going to do and why and in doing so you are articulating for the first time out loud the preference you have always had but never examined.
